frameworks.name: inverted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: population-level tuning model used to reconstruct stimuli from neural responses

frameworks.name: channel response profile reconstruction
frameworks.one_line: standard IEM evaluation via aligned/averaged reconstructions ignoring assumed basis set

frameworks.name: population tuning function decoding
frameworks.one_line: decoding framework grounded in neuronal tuning across continuous stimulus space

core_question: How can inverted encoding models be evaluated without confounding results with the encoder's assumed channel response profiles?
key_assumptions: population-level tuning functions reflect underlying neuronal tuning
decoding can occur on a continuous stimulus scale beyond trained values
standard reconstructions implicitly inherit the chosen basis set
manipulations: comparison of standard versus modified IEM evaluation
application to fMRI data from visual perception and attention studies
key_findings: standard IEM evaluations can produce spurious conclusions by ignoring assumed channel profiles
the proposed modification accounts for encoder basis set in reconstructions
modified method enables trial-by-trial goodness-of-fit estimates
interpretations: current IEM evaluation practices conflate basis-set assumptions with decoded content
accounting for channel response profiles improves decoding interpretability
